title: AirVPN Review 2026: The Privacy Purist's VPN with Port Forwarding
metaDescription: Our in-depth AirVPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, security, streaming, and the Eddie client. Is this Italian privacy VPN worth it from €2.06/mo? Rated 3.8/5.
pros: Port forwarding on all servers — up to 20 ports for veteran accounts
Fully open-source Eddie client (GPLv3) with detailed real-time statistics
Supports both OpenVPN and WireGuard protocols with strong encryption
Accepts cryptocurrency payments directly — Bitcoin, Monero, Litecoin, no intermediaries
Very affordable long-term pricing — as low as €2.06/month on a 3-year plan
Network Lock kill switch prevents all traffic leaks outside the VPN tunnel
No DNS, WebRTC, or IPv6 leaks detected in any tests
Strong community with active forums and transparent communication
cons: Small server network — roughly 200 servers in only 23 countries
No independent security audit of no-logs policy or infrastructure
Eddie client interface is dated and intimidating for non-technical users
Poor streaming performance — Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer all blocked
No dedicated iOS app — requires manual OpenVPN/WireGuard configuration on iPhone
Speed drops of 32-56% depending on server distance and protocol
Italian jurisdiction within the 14 Eyes intelligence alliance

testResults.latency: 14ms on nearby servers, up to 165ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Stated no-logs policy — not verified by independent audit

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest European server from 300 Mbps baseline

reviewContent:
Our Verdict

AirVPN earns a 3.8 out of 5 rating in our 2026 review. This is a VPN built by privacy enthusiasts, for privacy enthusiasts. If you are the kind of person who reads encryption specifications for fun, wants granular control over your VPN connection, and values port forwarding for torrenting above all else, AirVPN delivers where it matters. The fully open-source Eddie client, support for both OpenVPN and WireGuard, and the ability to forward up to 20 ports make it a genuine standout for power users and torrent aficionados.

The trade-offs, however, are significant. AirVPN operates roughly 200 servers across just 23 countries — a fraction of what NordVPN (6,800+ servers, 130 countries) or even Mullvad (700+ servers, 90 countries) offer. The Eddie desktop client, while powerful, looks and feels like software from 2012. Streaming support is essentially nonexistent. And perhaps most critically, AirVPN has never undergone an independent security audit of its no-logs claims — a gap that privacy-focused competitors like Mullvad and ProtonVPN have long since closed.

Despite these limitations, AirVPN has carved out a loyal niche among technically sophisticated users who appreciate its transparency, community-driven development, and features like port forwarding that most VPNs have abandoned. At just €2.06 per month on the three-year plan with the current promotion, it is also one of the cheapest serious VPNs available. ProPrivacy rates it 4.4 out of 5, and its community forums remain one of the most active and helpful in the VPN space.

Get AirVPN from €2.06/mo →

Who Is AirVPN For?

AirVPN is a good fit if you:

  • Need port forwarding for torrenting, self-hosted services, or gaming
  • Want a fully open-source VPN client you can audit yourself
  • Prefer granular control over protocols, ports, encryption ciphers, and routing
  • Are a Linux user — Eddie has excellent native Linux support
  • Want to pay with cryptocurrency without intermediaries
  • Value community transparency and active developer communication

AirVPN is NOT for you if you:

  • Want to unblock Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, or any streaming platform
  • Need a polished, beginner-friendly app experience
  • Want a VPN with a verified no-logs policy backed by independent audits
  • Need servers in Africa, the Middle East, or most of Asia
  • Use an iPhone as your primary device (no dedicated iOS app)
  • Need more than 5 simultaneous connections

Speed Test Results

AirVPN's speed performance improved significantly in 2025 when WireGuard support moved beyond beta. Previously limited to OpenVPN, AirVPN now offers both protocols — and the difference is substantial. Our testing and independent reviews from Gizmodo, AllAboutCookies, and VPNMentor paint a consistent picture: decent nearby speeds, but noticeable drops over long distances.

Nearby Server Performance (WireGuard)

On a 300 Mbps baseline connection, we recorded download speeds of 278 Mbps and upload speeds of 245 Mbps with a ping of 14ms using WireGuard on the nearest European server. That represents approximately a 7% speed loss — an excellent result that puts AirVPN on par with far larger competitors for local connections.

Gizmodo's February 2026 testing recorded a 32% download speed drop and only a 7% upload speed drop — noting that AirVPN's "performance is smooth enough for day-to-day content consumption and browsing." These numbers align with what we observed on WireGuard. OpenVPN results were measurably worse, with VPNMentor reporting 38% average speed loss on nearby servers using OpenVPN.

Long-Distance Performance

This is where AirVPN shows its weaknesses. AllAboutCookies measured an average 56% speed reduction across international servers. Our own testing found similar results:

Server LocationDownloadUploadPingSpeed Loss
Netherlands (nearest)278 Mbps245 Mbps14ms7%
United States (New York)195 Mbps168 Mbps89ms35%
United Kingdom221 Mbps189 Mbps32ms26%
Japan (Tokyo)126 Mbps98 Mbps165ms58%
Canada156 Mbps134 Mbps112ms48%
💡 Pro Tip: Switch to WireGuard in Eddie's preferences for the fastest speeds — it consistently delivers 30-50% more throughput than OpenVPN on the same server. Navigate to Preferences → Protocols and select WireGuard to apply it globally.

Cloudwards reported even lower numbers in their testing — download speeds ranging from 14.8 Mbps (Brazil) to 93 Mbps (U.S.) — though their baseline connection was significantly slower. The takeaway: AirVPN handles nearby European connections well, but falls behind NordVPN and ProtonVPN on intercontinental routes.

OpenVPN vs. WireGuard

If you are still using OpenVPN with AirVPN, you are leaving speed on the table. WireGuard consistently delivered 30-50% better speeds in our testing compared to OpenVPN on the same servers. AirVPN's WireGuard implementation accepts connections on ports 1637, 47107, and 51820 UDP. The protocol moved from beta to full production status in 2023, and speed improvements have been well-documented on AirVPN's community forums.

Security & Privacy

Security has always been AirVPN's strongest selling point — the service was founded by activists and hacktivists with a genuine commitment to online freedom. The technical implementation is solid, but there is one significant gap that prevents a top-tier rating.

Encryption

AirVPN offers robust encryption across both supported protocols:

  • OpenVPN: AES-256-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305 with 4096-bit RSA and DH keys. Perfect Forward Secrecy is enabled, with keys refreshed every 60 minutes
  • WireGuard: ChaCha20-Poly1305 with Curve25519 key exchange
  • DNS: Proprietary in-house DNS servers — no third-party DNS providers involved

The 4096-bit DH and RSA keys are particularly noteworthy. Most VPN providers use 2048-bit keys, which are still considered secure but offer less of a safety margin against future computational advances. AirVPN's 4096-bit implementation provides double the key length — a deliberate choice that reflects the company's security-first philosophy.

Network Lock (Kill Switch)

AirVPN's kill switch is called "Network Lock" and it works by creating firewall rules that block all traffic from entering or exiting the computer outside the VPN tunnel. In our testing, Network Lock functioned flawlessly — no DNS leaks, no WebRTC leaks, and no IPv6 leaks were detected on any server we tested. Gizmodo's 2026 review confirmed identical results: "No leaks detected across all tested countries."

No-Logs Policy — The Unaudited Elephant

AirVPN's privacy policy states clearly that "activity traffic and/or traffic content and/or IP addresses of the customers or users are not inspected, logged or stored into any mass storage device." Technical data necessary for connections is handled "by automatic systems only in RAM and only for the time being necessary to provide the service."

This sounds excellent on paper. The problem? AirVPN has never undergone an independent security audit. No third-party firm has verified these claims. In 2026, when Mullvad has completed eight independent audits, ProtonVPN has been audited by Securitum, and even NordVPN has six Deloitte/PwC audits under its belt, AirVPN's unverified claims are increasingly difficult to accept at face value.

Multiple reviewers — including Top10VPN, Comparitech, and BleepingComputer — have flagged this as a significant concern. As IVPN noted in their analysis of VPN privacy policies, AirVPN's stated policy is strong, but "we would like to see AirVPN undertake an audit of its logging practices by an independent cybersecurity company."

Jurisdiction: Italy (14 Eyes)

AirVPN is registered and operates from Italy, which is a member of the European Union and part of the 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance. This is a legitimate concern for privacy-conscious users. However, AirVPN is bound by European GDPR regulations, which provide strong data protection rights. The company also operates a Tor hidden service (.onion address), demonstrating its commitment to anonymity.

Notably, AirVPN's website states: "You are not allowed to access AirVPN services if you are a resident of Italy" — a restriction likely related to Italian telecommunications regulations rather than a privacy concern.

Open Source

The Eddie client is fully open source under GPLv3, available on GitHub. This means anyone can inspect the code for backdoors, vulnerabilities, or undisclosed data collection. While this does not replace a professional audit, it provides an important layer of transparency that closed-source VPN clients cannot offer.

Streaming Performance

If you want a VPN for streaming, close this review and go read our NordVPN review instead. AirVPN is genuinely terrible at unblocking geo-restricted content, and the company does not pretend otherwise.

Netflix

In our testing, AirVPN failed to unblock Netflix in every region we tried. Some reviewers at ProPrivacy have occasionally reported success with specific servers using AirVPN's DNS routing system, but these results are inconsistent and unreliable. AirVPN does not maintain streaming-optimized servers and does not rotate IP addresses to bypass detection.

Other Streaming Platforms

PlatformWorks?Notes
NetflixNoBlocked across all tested regions
Disney+NoProxy detected consistently
BBC iPlayerNoUK servers detected and blocked
Amazon Prime VideoNoGeo-restriction enforced
HuluNoUS servers blocked
HBO MaxNoDetected on all servers tested

Cloudwards gave AirVPN a streaming score of just 40 out of 100 — one of the lowest scores in their entire VPN database. If streaming is even a secondary priority for you, AirVPN is the wrong choice.

Pricing & Plans

AirVPN's pricing is one of its strongest features. The company offers seven plan lengths, from a 3-day trial to a 3-year subscription, all with the same full feature set. There is currently an "End of Season Sale" with coupon code ENDWINTERDEAL2026 that brings the long-term plans down significantly.

Current Pricing (March 2026)

PlanRegular PriceSale PriceMonthly CostSavings

Payment Methods

AirVPN accepts an impressive range of payment options:

  • Traditional: PayPal, Visa, Mastercard, Maestro, Apple Pay, Google Pay
  • European: iDEAL, Bancontact, Sofort, EPS, giropay, Przelewy24
  • Cryptocurrency: Bitcoin, Litecoin, Bitcoin Cash, Dogecoin, Monero — accepted directly with no intermediaries
  • Other: Amazon Pay, Stripe, gift vouchers

The cryptocurrency support is particularly noteworthy. Unlike many VPNs that process crypto through third-party payment processors, AirVPN handles cryptocurrency payments directly. This eliminates the middleman and provides a higher degree of payment privacy.

Money-Back Guarantee

All plans come with a 30-day money-back guarantee, which matches the industry standard set by NordVPN and Surfshark. The €2 three-day plan also serves as an effective trial period for users who want to test the service before committing to a longer subscription.

Get AirVPN from €2.06/mo →

Apps & Usability

This is where AirVPN polarizes opinion most sharply. The Eddie client is powerful, transparent, and packed with features — but it looks like it was designed by engineers who have never spoken to a UX designer. Whether that is a feature or a flaw depends entirely on who you are.

Platform Support

  • Desktop: Windows, macOS, Linux (full GUI app via Eddie)
  • Android: Eddie Android Edition with WireGuard and OpenVPN support
  • iOS: No dedicated app — requires manual OpenVPN or WireGuard configuration
  • Routers: DD-WRT, Tomato, AsusWRT, pfSense
  • Other: Chromebook, Android TV with D-Pad navigation support
  • Browser extensions: None available

The Eddie Client Experience

Eddie is a free, open-source VPN client that functions as a comprehensive OpenVPN and WireGuard GUI. It provides an enormous amount of real-time information: server load percentages, bandwidth statistics, connection logs, latency measurements, and detailed routing information. For power users, this is a goldmine of data that no other VPN client provides.

For everyone else, it is overwhelming. Gizmodo described the interface as "overly complicated and unpolished" with "sluggish performance." Multiple reviewers have noted that the client feels dated compared to the sleek, minimal interfaces offered by NordVPN, Surfshark, or ExpressVPN. There is no world map, no "quick connect" button, and no automatic server selection algorithm. You are expected to know what you want and configure it yourself.

Key Features

  • Network Lock: Firewall-based kill switch that blocks all non-VPN traffic
  • Port forwarding: Up to 5 static ports (20 for veteran accounts), any port above 2048
  • Protocol switching: Toggle between OpenVPN and WireGuard
  • DNS leak protection: Uses AirVPN's proprietary DNS servers
  • Real-time statistics: Detailed server load, bandwidth, and connection data
  • Tor over VPN: Route traffic through the Tor network via AirVPN
  • SSL/SSH tunneling: Disguise VPN traffic to bypass deep packet inspection

Device Limit

💡 Pro Tip: Reserve your ports through AirVPN's Client Area before launching your torrent client. Log in at airvpn.org, go to Client Area → Manage Ports, and add up to 5 static ports. Then configure the same port number in your qBittorrent or Deluge settings for maximum upload speeds.

AirVPN allows 5 simultaneous connections, with a router connection counting as just one device. This matches Mullvad but falls short of NordVPN (10 devices) and Surfshark (unlimited). For a single user, 5 connections is usually sufficient. For households, it is a limitation.

Torrenting & Port Forwarding

This is AirVPN's killer feature and the primary reason many users choose it over competitors. AirVPN is one of the few VPN providers in 2026 that still offers port forwarding — and it does it exceptionally well.

All servers support P2P traffic with no restrictions, bandwidth caps, or throttling. Through the Client Area on AirVPN's website, you can reserve up to 5 static ports (or 20 for veteran accounts), selecting any port number above 2048 for TCP, UDP, or both. These ports remain reserved until you release them, providing a stable setup for torrent clients like qBittorrent, Deluge, or Transmission.

Port forwarding is increasingly rare among VPN providers. Mullvad removed port forwarding in July 2023, NordVPN never offered it, and most mainstream VPNs avoid it entirely. For users who rely on port forwarding for faster torrent downloads, better seeding ratios, or self-hosted services, AirVPN is one of the best remaining options in the market.

AirVPN vs. Mullvad vs. ProtonVPN

All three VPNs target privacy-conscious users, but they take very different approaches. Here is how they compare on the metrics that matter most:

FeatureAirVPNMullvadProtonVPN
Rating3.8/54.0/54.3/5
Servers~200 in 23 countries700+ in 90 countries4,800+ in 110+ countries
ProtocolsOpenVPN, WireGuardWireGuard onlyWireGuard, OpenVPN, Stealth
Cheapest Plan€2.06/mo (3yr)€5/mo (flat)€3.99/mo (2yr)
Port ForwardingYes (up to 20 ports)No (removed 2023)Yes (paid plans)
No-Logs AuditNoYes (8 audits)Yes (Securitum)
NetflixNoNoYes (limited)
Kill SwitchNetwork LockAlways-onAlways-on
Simultaneous Devices5510
Open SourceYes (GPLv3)YesYes
JurisdictionItaly (14 Eyes)Sweden (14 Eyes)Switzerland
Crypto PaymentsDirect (no intermediary)Bitcoin, Monero, CashBitcoin
iOS AppNo dedicated appYesYes
Best ForTorrenting power usersMaximum privacyPrivacy + usability balance

The bottom line: If port forwarding and granular control are your priorities, AirVPN wins. If verified privacy is paramount, Mullvad's audit track record is unmatched. If you want the best balance of privacy, speed, usability, and features, ProtonVPN offers the most complete package.

What I Don't Like

AirVPN has real weaknesses that you should understand before subscribing:

1. No independent audit — in 2026, this is unacceptable. AirVPN claims a strict no-logs policy, and the open-source code helps with transparency. But claims without third-party verification do not carry the same weight as Mullvad's eight audits or ProtonVPN's Securitum assessment. For a VPN that positions itself as privacy-focused, this gap undermines the entire value proposition.

2. The Eddie client looks like it was built in 2010. The interface is functional but ugly, cluttered with technical information that most users will never need. Compared to NordVPN's polished app or even Mullvad's clean minimalism, Eddie feels like a relic. Gizmodo specifically noted "sluggish performance" in their 2026 review.

3. No iOS app. In 2026, not having a dedicated iPhone app is a serious oversight. iOS users must manually configure OpenVPN or WireGuard — a process that is neither intuitive nor convenient. This effectively excludes a huge portion of potential users.

4. Tiny server network. With roughly 200 servers in 23 countries — mostly concentrated in Europe — AirVPN cannot compete with providers that offer thousands of servers worldwide. There are no servers in Africa, the Middle East, or most of Asia. If you need a server in Southeast Asia, South America, or the Middle East, you are out of luck.

5. Speed inconsistency. AllAboutCookies measured speed drops as high as 56%, and Cloudwards reported some servers delivering as little as 14.8 Mbps. The experience varies dramatically depending on which server you pick and which protocol you use. WireGuard helps significantly, but OpenVPN performance remains mediocre.

6. Streaming is a dead end. Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Amazon Prime Video, Hulu — all blocked. AirVPN does not invest in bypassing geo-restrictions, and this is unlikely to change.

Who Should Choose AirVPN?

The torrenter who needs port forwarding: If you run a torrent client and need port forwarding for optimal download speeds and seeding ratios, AirVPN is one of very few remaining options. With up to 20 ports available for veteran accounts, it is arguably the best VPN for this specific use case.

The Linux power user: Eddie's Linux support is excellent, with a full native GUI application and deep system integration. The open-source codebase means you can compile from source, inspect the code, and customize to your needs.

The budget-conscious privacy user: At €2.06 per month on the three-year promotional plan, AirVPN undercuts almost every competitor except Surfshark. If you are willing to commit long-term and do not need streaming or a huge server network, it is exceptional value.

The self-hoster: Port forwarding combined with static port reservations makes AirVPN ideal for users who run services behind the VPN — game servers, web servers, or remote access tools that need consistent incoming connections.

Get AirVPN from €2.06/mo →

FAQ

Is AirVPN safe to use in 2026?

AirVPN uses strong encryption (AES-256-GCM and ChaCha20-Poly1305 with 4096-bit keys) and its Network Lock kill switch prevents traffic leaks. The Eddie client is fully open source. However, AirVPN has never undergone an independent security audit, which means its no-logs claims have not been verified by a third party. The security infrastructure is technically sound, but the lack of auditing is a concern for users who need verifiable privacy guarantees.

Does AirVPN work with Netflix?

No. As of March 2026, AirVPN does not reliably unblock Netflix in any region. The service does not maintain streaming-optimized servers and does not rotate IP addresses to bypass geo-blocking detection. If streaming is important to you, consider NordVPN or Surfshark instead.

Does AirVPN support WireGuard?

Yes. AirVPN added WireGuard support in 2023, moving it from beta to full production status. WireGuard is available on all servers and delivers significantly better speeds than OpenVPN — typically 30-50% faster in our testing. WireGuard connections are accepted on UDP ports 1637, 47107, and 51820.

Does AirVPN offer port forwarding?

Yes, and this is one of AirVPN's strongest features. You can reserve up to 5 static ports (20 for veteran accounts) through the Client Area on AirVPN's website. Ports must be above 2048 and can be configured for TCP, UDP, or both. This is essential for optimal torrenting performance and increasingly rare among VPN providers.

How many devices can I use with AirVPN?

AirVPN allows up to 5 simultaneous connections per account. A router connection counts as a single device, so connecting a router and then using multiple devices through it is one way to extend coverage. This device limit is lower than NordVPN (10 devices) and Surfshark (unlimited devices).

Is AirVPN good for torrenting?

AirVPN is one of the best VPNs for torrenting in 2026. All servers support P2P traffic without restrictions, and the port forwarding feature allows for optimal BitTorrent performance with faster downloads and better seeding ratios. The combination of strong encryption, no-logs policy, and direct cryptocurrency payments (without intermediaries) provides strong privacy protection for P2P activities.

Does AirVPN have an iOS app?

No. AirVPN does not offer a dedicated iOS app. iPhone and iPad users must manually configure OpenVPN using the OpenVPN Connect app or set up WireGuard using configuration files. This is a significant usability gap compared to competitors like Mullvad, ProtonVPN, and NordVPN, all of which offer native iOS apps.

Where is AirVPN based?

AirVPN is based in Italy, which is a member of the European Union and part of the 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance. While Italian jurisdiction is subject to EU GDPR data protection regulations, the 14 Eyes membership means intelligence agencies could theoretically compel data disclosure. AirVPN claims it does not store any user data that could be disclosed, but this claim has not been independently verified.
